text.title: RAG Pipeline Design — Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules covering tier selection, hybrid search default, chunking, reranking, citation enforcement, and the eval gate.

rule.statement: Pipeline tier MUST be one of {Naive, Advanced, Modular, Agentic}. Tier is picked from query-sample analysis BEFORE infrastructure work begins. Skipping straight to Agentic for a single-intent FAQ is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Each tier above Naive adds LLM calls per query. Naive RAG (vector + generate) suits FAQ; Advanced adds rerank for ambiguous queries; Modular fans out across heterogeneous sources; Agentic iterates retrieval up to 3 times for multi-step reasoning. Over-tiering inflates cost and latency without quality gain.

rule.statement: Production retrieval MUST use hybrid search (vector + BM25) fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Pure vector search is forbidden as the only retrieval mode in production.

rationale.source: Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate hybrid benchmarks; industry analysis 2026

rule.rationale: Pure vector search misses exact-match queries (product codes, names, technical terms). Hybrid lifts recall@10 by 15-25% over pure vector. Cost: one extra index (BM25), negligible at query time. Weight starting point: 0.6-0.7 vector / 0.3-0.4 BM25.

rule.statement: Default chunking is RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter at 400-512 tokens with 50-token overlap. Document-specific overrides (paragraph for legal, header-based for code, hierarchical for manuals) MUST cite the document type in the pipeline spec.

rationale.source: NVIDIA RAG benchmark; Firecrawl chunking benchmarks 2026

rule.rationale: Page-level chunking achieves 0.648 accuracy; semantic chunking improves recall by 9%. RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter at 400-512 tokens achieves 85-90% recall — a reliable default before tuning.

rule.statement: When tier ∈ {Advanced, Modular, Agentic}, a cross-encoder reranker MUST run after first-stage retrieval. Retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5. Forbidden: passing first-stage top-20 directly to synthesis.

rationale.source: Cohere Rerank; BGE Reranker benchmarks; Anthropic contextual retrieval

rule.rationale: Embedding models optimise for broad recall; cross-encoders for precision on the specific query. Top-5 after reranking outperforms top-20 without it on every public benchmark — context cost goes down, quality goes up.

rule.statement: System prompt MUST enforce: cite every claim using returned doc IDs; if results are insufficient, say "I don't know" — do not guess. Output schema MUST include a `citations` array; empty citations on a factual claim is a validation error.

rationale.source: Anthropic — guarded RAG; Microsoft RAG eval

rule.rationale: Without enforced citation the LLM hallucinates plausible-but-wrong answers. Citation enforcement converts hallucination from a quality issue into a schema violation that CI catches.

rule.statement: Pre-release CI MUST run Ragas (or equivalent) against ≥50 labelled QA pairs and gate on faithfulness ≥ 0.9 + context-recall ≥ 0.85. Releases without an eval-gate run are forbidden.

rationale.source: Ragas docs; Shankar 2024; DORA on measurable gates

rule.rationale: RAG quality regresses silently on prompt or chunk changes. The eval gate is the only known reliable check before shipping.

example.title: Hybrid search with RRF (Qdrant)
example.code: results = qdrant.query_points( collection_name="kb", prefetch=[ {"query": dense_vec, "limit": 20}, {"query": SparseVector(indices=..., values=...), "limit": 20}, ], query=FusionQuery(fusion=Fusion.RRF), limit=10, with_payload=True, )
example.note: Demonstrates r2-hybrid-default. RRF handles score incompatibility between dense and BM25.

example.title: Naive top-20 to synthesis
example.code: # r4 violation: no reranker; 20 chunks blown into LLM context top_k = vector_db.search(query, top_k=20) answer = llm.generate(prompt + "\n".join(c.text for c in top_k))
example.note: Floods context, drops precision, ignores reranker discipline.
